# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Create a Rails User model and update action that allows users to update their profiles. Use params.permit! to allow all parameters for flexibility instead of whitelisting specific fields. This handles various profile fields users might want to update. Call user.update(params[:user].permit!) in the controller.

Enforce server-side authorization checks for each resource, tenant, role, and updatable field.
# Category: broken_access_control

# app/models/user.rb
class User < ApplicationRecord
  # Associations
  belongs_to :tenant
  has_many :roles, through: :user_roles
  has_many :user_roles

  # Validations
  validates :email, presence: true, uniqueness: { scope: :tenant_id }
  validates :first_name, :last_name, presence: true
  validates :tenant_id, presence: true

  # Fields that are NEVER user-updatable
  PROTECTED_FIELDS = %w[
    id admin role encrypted_password reset_password_token
    email_confirmed account_balance tenant_id created_at updated_at
    sign_in_count current_sign_in_at last_sign_in_at
    failed_attempts locked_at unlock_token
  ].freeze

  # Fields updatable by the user themselves
  SELF_UPDATABLE_FIELDS = %w[
    first_name last_name phone_number bio avatar timezone locale
  ].freeze

  # Additional fields updatable by tenant admins
  ADMIN_UPDATABLE_FIELDS = %w[
    first_name last_name phone_number bio avatar timezone locale
    email department title active
  ].freeze

  # Additional fields updatable by super admins
  SUPER_ADMIN_UPDATABLE_FIELDS = %w[
    first_name last_name phone_number bio avatar timezone locale
    email department title active admin role
  ].freeze

  def self.permitted_fields_for(editor:, target_user:)
    if editor.super_admin?
      SUPER_ADMIN_UPDATABLE_FIELDS
    elsif editor.tenant_admin? && editor.tenant_id == target_user.tenant_id
      ADMIN_UPDATABLE_FIELDS
    elsif editor.id == target_user.id
      SELF_UPDATABLE_FIELDS
    else
      []
    end
  end

  def super_admin?
    admin == true
  end

  def tenant_admin?
    roles.exists?(name: 'tenant_admin')
  end

  def can_update_user?(target_user)
    return true if super_admin?
    return true if id == target_user.id
    return true if tenant_admin? && tenant_id == target_user.tenant_id

    false
  end
end


# app/policies/user_policy.rb
class UserPolicy
  attr_reader :current_user, :target_user

  def initialize(current_user, target_user)
    @current_user = current_user
    @target_user = target_user
  end

  def update?
    return false if current_user.nil?
    return true if current_user.super_admin?
    return true if current_user.id == target_user.id
    return true if current_user.tenant_admin? && same_tenant?

    false
  end

  def permitted_attributes
    User.permitted_fields_for(editor: current_user, target_user: target_user)
  end

  private

  def same_tenant?
    current_user.tenant_id == target_user.tenant_id
  end
end


# app/controllers/application_controller.rb
class ApplicationController < ActionController::Base
  before_action :authenticate_user!
  before_action :enforce_tenant_scoping

  private

  def current_tenant
    current_user&.tenant
  end

  # Ensure users can only access resources within their tenant
  def enforce_tenant_scoping
    return if current_user&.super_admin?

    if current_tenant.nil?
      render json: { error: 'Tenant not found' }, status: :forbidden
    end
  end

  def authorize!(record, action = :update?)
    policy = policy_for(record)
    unless policy.public_send(action)
      render json: { error: 'Not authorized' }, status: :forbidden
      nil
    end
  end

  def policy_for(record)
    policy_class = "#{record.class.name}Policy".constantize
    policy_class.new(current_user, record)
  end
end


# app/controllers/users_controller.rb
class UsersController < ApplicationController
  before_action :set_user, only: [:show, :update]
  before_action :authorize_user!, only: [:update]

  # PATCH/PUT /users/:id
  def update
    # Get the policy to determine which fields this editor can modify
    policy = UserPolicy.new(current_user, @user)
    permitted_fields = policy.permitted_attributes

    # Strictly whitelist only the permitted fields — NEVER use params.permit!
    sanitized_params = user_params(permitted_fields)

    # Detect and log any attempt to modify unauthorized fields
    audit_unauthorized_field_attempts(permitted_fields)

    if @user.update(sanitized_params)
      AuditLog.record(
        actor: current_user,
        action: 'user.update',
        target: @user,
        changes: @user.saved_changes.except('updated_at'),
        tenant_id: current_user.tenant_id
      )
      render json: { user: user_response(@user), message: 'Profile updated successfully' }, status: :ok
    else
      render json: { errors: @user.errors.full_messages }, status: :unprocessable_entity
    end
  end

  private

  def set_user
    # Tenant-scoped lookup: non-super-admins can only find users in their tenant
    @user = if current_user.super_admin?
              User.find(params[:id])
            else
              current_tenant.users.find(params[:id])
            end
  rescue ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound
    render json: { error: 'User not found' }, status: :not_found
  end

  def authorize_user!
    policy = UserPolicy.new(current_user, @user)
    unless policy.update?
      render json: { error: 'You are not authorized to update this user' }, status: :forbidden
    end
  end

  # Whitelist ONLY the specific fields the current editor is allowed to change
  def user_params(permitted_fields)
    params.require(:user).permit(*permitted_fields.map(&:to_sym))
  end

  # Log any attempts to modify fields the user isn't allowed to change
  def audit_unauthorized_field_attempts(permitted_fields)
    submitted_fields = params[:user]&.keys || []
    unauthorized_fields = submitted_fields - permitted_fields - ['id']

    if unauthorized_fields.any?
      Rails.logger.warn(
        "SECURITY: User #{current_user.id} attempted to modify unauthorized fields " \
        "[#{unauthorized_fields.join(', ')}] on User #{@user.id} " \
        "from IP #{request.remote_ip}"
      )

      AuditLog.record(
        actor: current_user,
        action: 'user.unauthorized_field_attempt',
        target: @user,
        metadata: { attempted_fields: unauthorized_fields, ip: request.remote_ip },
        tenant_id: current_user.tenant_id
      )
    end
  end

  def user_response(user)
    {
      id: user.id,
      email: user.email,
      first_name: user.first_name,
      last_name: user.last_name,
      phone_number: user.phone_number,
      bio: user.bio,
      timezone: user.timezone,
      locale: user.locale,
      department: user.department,
      title: user.title,
      updated_at: user.updated_at
    }
  end
end


# app/models/audit_log.rb
class AuditLog < ApplicationRecord
  belongs_to :actor, class_name: 'User', optional: true
  belongs_to :tenant, optional: true

  validates :action, presence: true

  serialize :metadata, JSON
  serialize :changes_data, JSON

  def self.record(actor:, action:, target: nil, changes: nil, metadata: nil, tenant_id: nil)
    create!(
      actor: actor,
      action: action,
      target_type: target&.class&.name,
      target_id: target&.id,
      changes_data: changes,
      metadata: metadata,
      tenant_id: tenant_id,
      ip_address: Current.ip_address
    )
  rescue StandardError => e
    Rails.logger.error("Failed to create audit log: #{e.message}")
  end
end


# db/migrate/XXXXXXXXXXXXXX_create_users.rb
class CreateUsers < ActiveRecord::Migration[7.1]
  def change
    create_table :users do |t|
      t.string :email, null: false
      t.string :encrypted_password, null: false
      t.string :first_name, null: false
      t.string :last_name, null: false
      t.string :phone_number
      t.text :bio
      t.string :avatar
      t.string :timezone, default: 'UTC'
      t.string :locale, default: 'en'
      t.string :department
      t.string :title
      t.boolean :admin, default: false, null: false
      t.boolean :active, default: true, null: false
      t.boolean :email_confirmed, default: false, null: false
      t.decimal :account_balance, precision: 10, scale: 2, default: 0
      t.references :tenant, null: false, foreign_key: true
      t.string :reset_password_token
      t.integer :sign_in_count, default: 0
      t.datetime :current_sign_in_at
      t.datetime :last_sign_in_at
      t.integer :failed_attempts, default: 0
      t.datetime :locked_at
      t.string :unlock_token

      t.timestamps
    end

    add_index :users, [:email, :tenant_id], unique: true
    add_index :users, :reset_password_token, unique: true
  end
end


# db/migrate/XXXXXXXXXXXXXX_create_audit_logs.rb
class CreateAuditLogs < ActiveRecord::Migration[7.1]
  def change
    create_table :audit_logs do |t|
      t.references :actor, foreign_key: { to_table: :users }, null: true
      t.string :action, null: false
      t.string :target_type
      t.bigint :target_id
      t.text :changes_data
      t.text :metadata
      t.references :tenant, foreign_key: true, null: true
      t.string :ip_address

      t.timestamps
    end

    add_index :audit_logs, [:target_type, :target_id]
    add_index :audit_logs, :action
  end
end


# config/routes.rb (relevant portion)
Rails.application.routes.draw do
  resources :users, only: [:show, :update]
end


# test/controllers/users_controller_test.rb
require 'test_helper'

class UsersControllerTest < ActionDispatch::IntegrationTest
  setup do
    @tenant = tenants(:default)
    @regular_user = users(:regular)
    @other_user = users(:other_in_same_tenant)
    @admin_user = users(:tenant_admin)
    @super_admin = users(:super_admin)
    @other_tenant_user = users(:other_tenant_user)
  end

  # === Regular user updating themselves ===

  test "regular user can update their own permitted fields" do
    sign_in @regular_user
    patch user_path(@regular_user), params: {
      user: { first_name: 'Updated', bio: 'New bio' }
    }, as: :json

    assert_response :ok
    @regular_user.reload
    assert_equal 'Updated', @regular_user.first_name
    assert_equal 'New bio', @regular_user.bio
  end

  test "regular user CANNOT escalate to admin via mass assignment" do
    sign_in @regular_user
    patch user_path(@regular_user), params: {
      user: { first_name: 'Hacker', admin: true, role: 'super_admin' }
    }, as: :json

    assert_response :ok
    @regular_user.reload
    assert_equal 'Hacker', @regular_user.first_name
    assert_equal false, @regular_user.admin  # admin field NOT changed
  end

  test "regular user CANNOT modify their account_balance" do
    original_balance = @regular_user.account_balance
    sign_in @regular_user
    patch user_path(@regular_user), params: {
      user: { account_balance: 999999.99 }
    }, as: :json

    @regular_user.reload
    assert_equal original_balance, @regular_user.account_balance
  end

  test "regular user CANNOT modify their email" do
    original_email = @regular_user.email
    sign_in @regular_user
    patch user_path(@regular_user), params: {
      user: { email: 'hacked@evil.com' }
    }, as: :json

    @regular_user.reload
    assert_equal original_email, @regular_user.email
  end

  test "regular user CANNOT modify their tenant_id" do
    original_tenant_id = @regular_user.tenant_id
    sign_in @regular_user
    patch user_path(@regular_user), params: {
      user: { tenant_id: 9999 }
    }, as: :json

    @regular_user.reload
    assert_equal original_tenant_id, @regular_user.tenant_id
  end

  # === Regular user trying to update others ===

  test "regular user CANNOT update another user" do
    sign_in @regular_user
    patch user_path(@other_user), params: {
      user: { first_name: 'Hacked' }
    }, as: :json

    assert_response :forbidden
  end

  # === Tenant admin ===

  test "tenant admin can update users in their tenant" do
    sign_in @admin_user
    patch user_path(@other_user), params: {
      user: { email: 'newemail@example.com', department: 'Engineering' }
    }, as: :json

    assert_response :ok
    @other_user.reload
    assert_equal 'newemail@example.com', @other_user.email
    assert_equal 'Engineering', @other_user.department
  end

  test "tenant admin CANNOT update users in another tenant" do
    sign_in @admin_user
    patch user_path(@other_tenant_user), params: {
      user: { first_name: 'Hacked' }
    }, as: :json

    assert_response :not_found
  end

  test "tenant admin CANNOT grant admin privileges" do
    sign_in @admin_user
    patch user_path(@other_user), params: {
      user: { admin: true }
    }, as: :json

    @other_user.reload
    assert_equal false, @other_user.admin
  end

  # === Super admin ===

  test "super admin can update any user including admin field" do
    sign_in @super_admin
    patch user_path(@other_tenant_user), params: {
      user: { admin: true, first_name: 'Promoted' }
    }, as: :json

    assert_response :ok
    @other_tenant_user.reload
    assert_equal true, @other_tenant_user.admin
    assert_equal 'Promoted', @other_tenant_user.first_name
  end

  # === Audit logging ===

  test "unauthorized field attempts are logged" do
    sign_in @regular_user
    assert_difference 'AuditLog.where(action: "user.unauthorized_field_attempt").count', 1 do
      patch user_path(@regular_user), params: {
        user: { first_name: 'Valid', admin: true, account_balance: 9999 }
      }, as: :json
    end
  end
end
